3.why: We need the size to loop over the level-order layout.
3.invariant: values holds the tree in level order, with null for gaps.

4.happening: Creating an empty list of nodes.
4.why: This list is exactly what the stage will draw as the tree.

5.happening: Creating an empty by_id lookup map.
5.why: So we can fetch any node by its id in O(1).

6.why: state_by_id[i] tells the animation how to shade node i.

7.happening: Looping to initialise a colour state for each slot.
7.why: Every node must start idle before the walk begins.
7.invariant: state_by_id is being filled with zeros.

8.why: Idle is the neutral, un-highlighted look.
8.invariant: All states so far are idle.

9.happening: The child walker starts at slot 1.
9.why: Slot 0 is the root; its children begin right after it.
9.invariant: child points at the next unassigned slot.

10.happening: Second pass: building a node for each real slot.
10.why: Turning the flat array into linked node dicts.
10.invariant: Nodes are created in level order.

11.why: Only real values become tree nodes.

11.happening: Slot {i} is null — no node here.
11.why: A null marks a missing child in the level-order layout.
11.invariant: We skip gaps without advancing child.

12.why: Its left/right start as None until we link children.
12.invariant: The node's id equals its array index.

13.happening: Slot {child} is available for the left child.
13.why: We only read children while slots remain.

13.happening: No slots left — this node has no left child.
13.why: We are past the end of the array.

14.happening: Slot {child} = {values[child]} is a real left child.
14.why: A non-null slot means a genuine child link.

14.happening: Slot {child} is null — no left child.
14.why: A null child slot means this branch is empty.

15.why: Records the left edge for the drawn tree.
15.invariant: The left subtree hangs off this node.

16.happening: Advancing the child walker past the left slot.
16.why: The next slot belongs to the right child.

17.happening: Slot {child} is available for the right child.
17.why: We only read children while slots remain.

17.happening: No slots left — this node has no right child.
17.why: We are past the end of the array.

18.happening: Slot {child} = {values[child]} is a real right child.
18.why: A non-null slot means a genuine child link.

18.happening: Slot {child} is null — no right child.
18.why: A null child slot means this branch is empty.

19.why: Records the right edge for the drawn tree.
19.invariant: The right subtree hangs off this node.

20.happening: Advancing the child walker past the right slot.
20.why: Ready to build the next node's children.

21.happening: Adding node {i} to the drawn node list.
21.why: Once linked, the node joins the visible tree.

22.why: Enables O(1) lookup by id during the walk.

23.happening: The cursor starts at the root (node 0).
23.why: The BST walk always begins at the root.

24.why: It will be overwritten with the real LCA value.

25.why: It flips true only when we reach the split node.
25.invariant: The LCA is not located yet.

26.happening: Cursor is at a live node — keep walking.
26.why: We still have a node to compare against p and q.
26.invariant: current is a valid node id.

26.happening: No node left to visit — the walk ends.
26.why: The loop condition is now false.

27.why: We compare this value against p and q to pick a direction.
27.invariant: current_val is the value at node {current}.

28.happening: Highlighting node {current} as the one under inspection.
28.why: The animation shows exactly where the cursor sits.
28.invariant: Only the current node is state 'current'.

29.happening: Both p ({p}) and q ({q}) are smaller than {current_val}.
29.why: Both targets live in the smaller-valued left subtree.
29.invariant: The LCA cannot be to the right of here.

29.happening: p ({p}) and q ({q}) are NOT both smaller than {current_val}.
29.why: So the LCA is not strictly in the left subtree.
29.invariant: We fall through to the next test.

30.happening: Marking node {current} as passed before going left.
30.why: We have decided the LCA is deeper on the left.
30.invariant: This node is no longer the cursor.

31.happening: Cursor glides down to the left child.
31.why: Both targets are smaller, so we search left.
31.invariant: current now points at the left child.

32.happening: Both p ({p}) and q ({q}) are larger than {current_val}.
32.why: Both targets live in the larger-valued right subtree.
32.invariant: The LCA cannot be to the left of here.

32.happening: p ({p}) and q ({q}) are NOT both larger than {current_val}.
32.why: They straddle this node — the split is right here.

33.happening: Marking node {current} as passed before going right.
33.why: We have decided the LCA is deeper on the right.
33.invariant: This node is no longer the cursor.

34.happening: Cursor glides down to the right child.
34.why: Both targets are larger, so we search right.
34.invariant: current now points at the right child.

36.happening: About to record node {current} (value {current_val}) as the LCA.
36.why: p and q split here — one on each side, or one equals this node.
36.invariant: This is the lowest node with both as descendants.

37.why: The answer is now fixed — no need to search further.

38.why: The split node is the answer; deeper walking is pointless.

39.why: This is the lowest node that is an ancestor of both p and q.

init.happening: Rebuilding the BST from its level-order array.
init.why: We need the linked tree before we can walk it.
init.invariant: Setup runs once before the walk.

loop.happening: Checking whether the cursor still points at a node.
loop.why: The walk continues until a split node is found.
loop.invariant: current is the node under inspection.

check.happening: Comparing p and q against the current node's value.
check.why: The comparison decides left, right, or stop.

update.happening: Updating the current node's colour state.
update.why: So the animation reflects where the cursor is.

move.happening: Advancing the cursor to a child.
move.why: We descend toward the split point.
